source: Limitless (Bankless) — AI supercycle; Unity as world-model builder; chips/memory/power
summary: AI is in a supercycle, not a bubble — constrained by physical supply of watts and wafers, not debt-fueled speculation

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker explicitly named Google as one of the four hyperscalers funding AI buildout from free cash flow, which is the central evidence for the anti-bubble supercycle thesis.
Baker explicitly named Microsoft as one of the four hyperscalers whose FCF-funded AI capex underpins the supercycle argument.
Baker explicitly named Amazon as one of the four hyperscalers whose FCF-funded spending is cited as proof this is not a debt-fueled bubble.
Baker explicitly named Meta as one of the four hyperscalers whose FCF-funded capex is central to the supercycle thesis.

Baker specifically cited TSMC wafer capacity as one of the two physical supply constraints that make classic bubble overbuilding structurally impossible in this cycle — TSM is a direct subject of the supply-constraint argument.

While Nvidia is an obvious beneficiary of AI infrastructure spending, Baker did not specifically connect NVDA to this thesis about FCF-funded hyperscaler capex and physical supply constraints (wafers and watts). NVDA is a GPU supplier, not a wafer capacity or energy constraint. Its inclusion appears to be contamination from adjacent AI infrastructure discussion rather than a specific connection to this exact thesis.
